fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>date_time</code>: Date with time to subtract specified number of microseconds from. <a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a>/<a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>, <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li><li><code>num</code>: Number of microseconds to subtract. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">(U)Int*</a>, <a href="../data-types/float">Float*</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li>Returns <code>date_time</code> minus <code>num</code> microseconds. <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH
    toDateTime(&#x27;2024-01-01 00:00:00&#x27;) AS date_time,
    &#x27;2024-01-01 00:00:00&#x27; AS date_time_string
SELECT
    subtractMicroseconds(date_time, 1000000) AS subtract_microseconds_with_date_time,
    subtractMicroseconds(date_time_string, 1000000) AS subtract_microseconds_with_date_time_string
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─subtract_microseconds_with_date_time─┬─subtract_microseconds_with_date_time_string─┐
│           2023-12-31 23:59:59.000000 │                  2023-12-31 23:59:59.000000 │
└──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="subtractnanoseconds" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">subtractNanoseconds<a class="headerlink" href="#subtractnanoseconds" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Subtracts a specified number of nanoseconds from a datetime or string representation of a datetime.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">subtractNanoseconds(date_time, num)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>date_time</code>: Date with time to subtract specified number of nanoseconds from. <a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a>/<a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>, <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li><li><code>num</code>: Number of nanoseconds to subtract. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">(U)Int*</a>, <a href="../data-types/float">Float*</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li>Returns <code>date_time</code> minus <code>num</code> nanoseconds. <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH
    toDateTime(&#x27;2024-01-01 00:00:00&#x27;) AS date_time,
    &#x27;2024-01-01 00:00:00&#x27; AS date_time_string
SELECT
    subtractNanoseconds(date_time, 1000) AS subtract_nanoseconds_with_date_time,
    subtractNanoseconds(date_time_string, 1000) AS subtract_nanoseconds_with_date_time_string
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─subtract_nanoseconds_with_date_time─┬─subtract_nanoseconds_with_date_time_string─┐
│       2023-12-31 23:59:59.999999000 │              2023-12-31 23:59:59.999999000 │
└─────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="subtractinterval" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">subtractInterval<a class="headerlink" href="#subtractinterval" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Subtracts an interval from another interval or a tuple of intervals.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">subtractInterval(interval_1, interval_2)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>interval_1</code>: First interval or tuple of intervals. <a href="../data-types/interval">Interval</a>, <a href="../data-types/tuple">Tuple</a>(<a href="../data-types/interval">Interval</a>).</li><li><code>interval_2</code>: Second interval to be negated. <a href="../data-types/interval">Interval</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li>Returns a tuple of intervals. <a href="../data-types/tuple">Tuple</a>(<a href="../data-types/interval">Interval</a>).</li></ul><div class="my-4 flex items-start gap-2 py-3 px-4 rounded border border-v-border bg-v-callout-info text-code"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 16 16" class="inline shrink-0 mt-px h-5"><path fill="currentColor" d="M7.86 9.742a1 1 0 0 0-.08.1.5.5 0 0 0-.06.12.4.4 0 0 0-.04.12 1 1 0 0 0 0 .134.56.56 0 0 0 .053.253.6.6 0 0 0 .36.36.63.63 0 0 0 .507 0 .6.6 0 0 0 .36-.36.7.7 0 0 0 .04-.253.667.667 0 0 0-1.14-.474m.473-8.86a6.667 6.667 0 1 0 0 13.334 6.667 6.667 0 0 0 0-13.334m0 12a5.333 5.333 0 1 1 0-10.666 5.333 5.333 0 0 1 0 10.666m0-8.666a2 2 0 0 0-1.733 1 .667.667 0 1 0 1.153.666.667.667 0 0 1 1.052-.138.667.667 0 0 1-.472 1.138.667.667 0 0 0-.666.667v.667a.667.667 0 0 0 1.333 0v-.12a2 2 0 0 0-.667-3.88"></path></svg><div class="text-v-text"><p>Intervals of the same type will be combined into a single interval. For instance if <code>toIntervalDay(2)</code> and <code>toIntervalDay(1)</code> are passed then the result will be <code>(1)</code> rather than <code>(2,1)</code>.</p></div></div><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>Query:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT subtractInterval(INTERVAL 1 DAY, INTERVAL 1 MONTH);
SELECT subtractInterval((INTERVAL 1 DAY, INTERVAL 1 YEAR), INTERVAL 1 MONTH);
SELECT subtractInterval(INTERVAL 2 DAY, INTERVAL 1 DAY);
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─subtractInterval(toIntervalDay(1), toIntervalMonth(1))─┐
│ (1,-1)                                                 │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
┌─subtractInterval((toIntervalDay(1), toIntervalYear(1)), toIntervalMonth(1))─┐
│ (1,1,-1)                                                                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
┌─subtractInterval(toIntervalDay(2), toIntervalDay(1))─┐
│ (1)                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="subtracttupleofintervals" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">subtractTupleOfIntervals<a class="headerlink" href="#subtracttupleofintervals" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Applies a series of interval subtractions to a date or datetime value.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">subtractTupleOfIntervals(date, intervals)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>date</code>: Date or date with time. <a href="../data-types/date">Date</a>/<a href="../data-types/date32">Date32</a>/<a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a>/<a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li><li><code>intervals</code>: Tuple of intervals to subtract from <code>date</code>. <a href="../data-types/tuple">Tuple</a>(<a href="../data-types/interval">Interval</a>).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li>Returns <code>date</code> with subtracted <code>intervals</code>. <a href="../data-types/date">Date</a>/<a href="../data-types/date32">Date32</a>/<a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a>/<a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>Query:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH toDate(&#x27;2018-01-01&#x27;) AS date SELECT subtractTupleOfIntervals(date, (INTERVAL 1 DAY, INTERVAL 1 YEAR))
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─subtractTupleOfIntervals(date, (toIntervalDay(1), toIntervalYear(1)))─┐
│                                                            2016-12-31 │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="timeslots" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">timeSlots<a class="headerlink" href="#timeslots" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Generates an array of datetime values representing time slots within a specified duration, starting from a given time and rounded to a defined interval size.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">timeSlots(StartTime, Duration[, Size])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>StartTime</code>: The starting time for the interval. <a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a> or <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li><li><code>Duration</code>: The length of the time interval in seconds. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">UInt32</a> or <a href="../data-types/decimal">Decimal64</a>.</li><li><code>Size</code>: The size of each time slot in seconds (optional, default is 1800 for 30 minutes). <a href="../data-types/int-uint">UInt32</a> or <a href="../data-types/decimal">Decimal64</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li>An array of <a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a> or <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a> values.</li></ul><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT timeSlots(toDateTime(&#x27;2012-01-01 12:20:00&#x27;), toUInt32(600));
SELECT timeSlots(toDateTime(&#x27;1980-12-12 21:01:02&#x27;, &#x27;UTC&#x27;), toUInt32(600), 299);
SELECT timeSlots(toDateTime64(&#x27;1980-12-12 21:01:02.1234&#x27;, 4, &#x27;UTC&#x27;), toDecimal64(600.1, 1), toDecimal64(299, 0));
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─timeSlots(toDateTime(&#x27;2012-01-01 12:20:00&#x27;), toUInt32(600))─┐
│ [&#x27;2012-01-01 12:00:00&#x27;,&#x27;2012-01-01 12:30:00&#x27;]               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
┌─timeSlots(toDateTime(&#x27;1980-12-12 21:01:02&#x27;, &#x27;UTC&#x27;), toUInt32(600), 299)─┐
│ [&#x27;1980-12-12 20:56:13&#x27;,&#x27;1980-12-12 21:01:12&#x27;,&#x27;1980-12-12 21:06:11&#x27;]     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
┌─timeSlots(toDateTime64(&#x27;1980-12-12 21:01:02.1234&#x27;, 4, &#x27;UTC&#x27;), toDecimal64(600.1, 1), toDecimal64(299, 0))─┐
│ [&#x27;1980-12-12 20:56:13.0000&#x27;,&#x27;1980-12-12 21:01:12.0000&#x27;,&#x27;1980-12-12 21:06:11.0000&#x27;]                        │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="formatdatetime" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">formatDateTime<a class="headerlink" href="#formatdatetime" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Formats a date or datetime value into a string according to a specified format pattern, using MySQL-style format specifiers.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">formatDateTime(Time, Format[, Timezone])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Alias: <code>DATE_FORMAT</code>.</p><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>Time</code>: The date or time value to format. <a href="../data-types/date">Date</a>, <a href="../data-types/date32">Date32</a>, <a href="../data-types/datetime">DateTime</a> or <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li><li><code>Format</code>: A string literal defining the output format using MySQL-style specifiers. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li><li><code>Timezone</code>: Optional <a href="../data-types/string">Timezone</a> for the returned value.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li>Returns time and date values according to the determined format. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="replacement-fields" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Replacement fields<a class="headerlink" href="#replacement-fields" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>Using replacement fields, you can define a pattern for the resulting string. &quot;Example&quot; column shows formatting result for <code>2018-01-02 22:33:44</code>.</p><p>| Placeholder | Description</p></article><div class="flex items-center justify-end h-10" data-pagefind-ignore="true"><div class="italic text-caption"><span>Updated<!-- --> <time dateTime="2026-02-26T12:35:07.000Z">2 weeks ago</time></span></div></div><nav "1284\",\"static/chunks/1284-f30446673b9ba073.js\",\"7867\",\"static/chunks/7867-6d0484accd87a171.js\",\"3145\",\"static/chunks/3145-ca0c40eb1bb881d8.js\",\"5107\",\"static/chunks/5107-ef688f2fbbe55825.js\",\"2418\",\"static/chunks/2418-b0d27e6f47546ed6.js\",\"4144\",\"static/chunks/4144-34f98e6e70550e12.js\",\"1147\",\"static/chunks/1147-ac53c80b8f8a3921.js\",\"1749\",\"static/chunks/1749-b2df567f1b8974df.js\",\"5643\",\"static/chunks/5643-5ba78075e5c8bae2.js\",\"1796\",\"static/chunks/1796-b956fe6fe21dbf17.js\",\"787\",\"static/chunks/787-09fc1907ef45c351.js\",\"996\",\"static/chunks/996-ce0ceee74ea8f43a.js\",\"7432\",\"static/chunks/7432-03643de0998568d4.js\",\"5077\",\"static/chunks/app/(sql-reference)/sql-reference/%5B%5B...slug%5D%5D/page-1cd32edffe376f31.js\"],\"default\"]\n21:T20f94,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"---\ntitle: Date and Time Functions reference\nmeta:\n    description: Functions for manipulating dates and times.\nheadingMaxLevels: 2\n---\n\n# Functions for working with dates and times\n\nMost functions in this section accept an optional time zone argument, for example `Europe/Amsterdam`. In this case, the time zone is the specified one instead of the local or default one.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toDateTime('2016-06-15 23:00:00') AS time,\n    toDate(time) AS date_local,\n    toDate(time, 'Asia/Yekaterinburg') AS date_yekat,\n    toString(time, 'US/Samoa') AS time_samoa\n```\n\n```text\n┌────────────────time─┬─date_local─┬─date_yekat─┬─time_samoa──────────┐\n│ 2016-06-15 23:00:00 │ 2016-06-15 │ 2016-06-16 │ 2016-06-15 09:00:00 │\n└─────────────────────┴────────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## makeDate\n\nConstructs a `Date` value from individual year, month, and day components, or from a year and a day-of-year number.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nmakeDate(year, month, day)\nmakeDate(year, day_of_year)\n```\n\nAlias:\n- `MAKEDATE(year, month, day);`\n- `MAKEDATE(year, day_of_year);`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `year`: Year. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `month`: Month. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `day`: Day. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `day_of_year`: Day of the year. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n\n### Returns\n\n- A date created from the arguments. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\nCreate a Date from a year, month and day:\n\n```sql\nSELECT makeDate(2023, 2, 28) AS Date\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌───────date─┐\n│ 2023-02-28 │\n└────────────┘\n```\n\nCreate a Date from a year and day of year argument:\n\n```sql\nSELECT makeDate(2023, 42) AS Date\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌───────date─┐\n│ 2023-02-11 │\n└────────────┘\n```\n\n## makeDate32\n\nGenerates a `Date32` value from specified year, month, and day, or from a year and a day-of-year.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nmakeDate32(year, [month,] day)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `year`: Year. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `month`: Month (optional). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `day`: Day. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf `month` is omitted then `day` should take a value between `1` and `365`, otherwise it should take a value between `1` and `31`.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\n- A date created from the arguments. [Date32](../data-types/date32).\n\n### Example\n\nCreate a date from a year, month, and day:\n\n```sql\nSELECT makeDate32(2024, 1, 1)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n2024-01-01\n```\n\nCreate a Date from a year and day of year:\n\n```sql\nSELECT makeDate32(2024, 100)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n2024-04-09\n```\n\n## makeDateTime\n\nAssembles a `DateTime` value from its constituent year, month, day, hour, minute, and second parts.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nmakeDateTime(year, month, day, hour, minute, second[, timezone])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `year`: Year. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `month`: Month. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `day`: Day. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `hour`: Hour. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `minute`: Minute. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `second`: Second. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `timezone`: [Timezone](../data-types/string) for the returned value (optional).\n\n### Returns\n\n- A date with time created from the arguments. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT makeDateTime(2023, 2, 28, 17, 12, 33) AS DateTime\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌────────────DateTime─┐\n│ 2023-02-28 17:12:33 │\n└─────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## makeDateTime64\n\nConstructs a `DateTime64` value using provided year, month, day, hour, minute, and second, with an optional sub-second precision.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nmakeDateTime64(year, month, day, hour, minute, second[, precision])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `year`: Year (0-9999). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `month`: Month (1-12). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `day`: Day (1-31). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `hour`: Hour (0-23). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `minute`: Minute (0-59). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `second`: Second (0-59). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint), [Float](../data-types/float) or [Decimal](../data-types/decimal).\n- `precision`: Optional precision of the sub-second component (0-9). [Integer](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Returns\n\n- A date and time created from the supplied arguments. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT makeDateTime64(2023, 5, 15, 10, 30, 45, 779, 5)\n```\n\n```result\n┌─makeDateTime64(2023, 5, 15, 10, 30, 45, 779, 5)─┐\n│                       2023-05-15 10:30:45.00779 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## timestamp\n\nConverts a date or datetime expression into a `DateTime64(6)` value. An optional second argument can specify a time to add to the converted value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntimestamp(expr[, expr_time])\n```\n\nAlias: `TIMESTAMP`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: Date or date with time. [String](../data-types/string).\n- `expr_time`: Optional parameter. Time to add. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Returns\n\n- [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64)(6)\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT timestamp('2023-12-31') as ts\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─────────────────────────ts─┐\n│ 2023-12-31 00:00:00.000000 │\n└────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n```sql\nSELECT timestamp('2023-12-31 12:00:00', '12:00:00.11') as ts\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─────────────────────────ts─┐\n│ 2024-01-01 00:00:00.110000 │\n└────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## timeZone\n\nRetrieves the timezone configured for the current session.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntimeZone()\n```\n\nAlias: `timezone`.\n\n### Returns\n\n- Timezone. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT timezone()\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─timezone()─────┐\n│ America/Denver │\n└────────────────┘\n```\n\n## serverTimeZone\n\nProvides the timezone configured for the server.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nserverTimeZone()\n```\n\nAlias: `serverTimezone`.\n\n### Returns\n\n- Timezone. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT serverTimeZone()\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─serverTimeZone()─┐\n│ UTC              │\n└──────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toTimeZone\n\nChanges the timezone attribute of a `DateTime` or `DateTime64` value without altering its underlying Unix timestamp. This affects how the time is displayed.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoTimezone(value, timezone)\n```\n\nAlias: `toTimezone`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Time or date and time. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `timezone`: Timezone for the returned value. [String](../data-types/string). This argument is a constant, because `toTimezone` changes the timezone of a column (timezone is an attribute of `DateTime*` types).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Date and time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toDateTime('2019-01-01 00:00:00', 'UTC') AS time_utc,\n    toTypeName(time_utc) AS type_utc,\n    toInt32(time_utc) AS int32utc,\n    toTimeZone(time_utc, 'Asia/Yekaterinburg') AS time_yekat,\n    toTypeName(time_yekat) AS type_yekat,\n    toInt32(time_yekat) AS int32yekat,\n    toTimeZone(time_utc, 'US/Samoa') AS time_samoa,\n    toTypeName(time_samoa) AS type_samoa,\n    toInt32(time_samoa) AS int32samoa\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\nRow 1:\n──────\ntime_utc:   2019-01-01 00:00:00\ntype_utc:   DateTime('UTC')\nint32utc:   1546300800\ntime_yekat: 2019-01-01 05:00:00\ntype_yekat: DateTime('Asia/Yekaterinburg')\nint32yekat: 1546300800\ntime_samoa: 2018-12-31 13:00:00\ntype_samoa: DateTime('US/Samoa')\nint32samoa: 1546300800\n```\n\n## timeZoneOf\n\nExtracts the timezone name associated with a `DateTime` or `DateTime64` value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntimeZoneOf(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `timezoneOf`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Date and time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Timezone name. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT timezoneOf(now())\n```\n\nResult:\n```text\n┌─timezoneOf(now())─┐\n│ Etc/UTC           │\n└───────────────────┘\n```\n\n## timeZoneOffset\n\nCalculates the offset in seconds from UTC for a given `DateTime` or `DateTime64` value, considering daylight saving time and historical timezone changes.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntimeZoneOffset(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `timezoneOffset`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Date and time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Offset from UTC in seconds. [Int32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toDateTime('2021-04-21 10:20:30', 'America/New_York') AS Time, toTypeName(Time) AS Type,\n       timeZoneOffset(Time) AS Offset_in_seconds, (Offset_in_seconds / 3600) AS Offset_in_hours\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌────────────────Time─┬─Type─────────────────────────┬─Offset_in_seconds─┬─Offset_in_hours─┐\n│ 2021-04-21 10:20:30 │ DateTime('America/New_York') │            -14400 │              -4 │\n└─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴───────────────────┴─────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toYear\n\nExtracts the year component from a date or datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoYear(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `YEAR`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The year of the given date/time. [UInt16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                      2023 │\n└───────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toQuarter\n\nRetrieves the quarter of the year (1-4) from a date or datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoQuarter(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `QUARTER`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The quarter of the year (1, 2, 3 or 4) of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toQuarter(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toQuarter(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                            2 │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toMonth\n\nExtracts the month number (1-12) from a date or datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoMonth(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `MONTH`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The month of the year (1 - 12) of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                          4 │\n└────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toDayOfYear\n\nReturns the day number within the year (1 to 366) for a given date or datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoDayOfYear(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `DAYOFYEAR`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The day of the year (1 - 366) of the given date/time. [UInt16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toDayOfYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toDayOfYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                            111 │\n└────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toDayOfMonth\n\nExtracts the day number within its month (1 to 31) from a date or datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoDayOfMonth(value)\n```\n\nAliases: `DAYOFMONTH`, `DAY`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The day of the month (1 - 31) of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toDayOfMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toDayOfMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                              21 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toDayOfWeek\n\nDetermines the day of the week for a given date or datetime. You can specify the week's starting day and the numbering range (0-6 or 1-7) using an optional mode argument, and also provide a timezone.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoDayOfWeek(t[, mode[, timezone]])\n```\n\nAlias: `DAYOFWEEK`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `t`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `mode`: determines what the first day of the week is. Possible values are 0, 1, 2 or 3. See the table below for the differences.\n- `timezone`: optional parameter, it behaves like any other conversion function.\n\n| Mode | First day of week | Range                                          |\n|------|-------------------|------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Monday            | 1-7: Monday = 1, Tuesday = 2, ..., Sunday = 7  |\n| 1    | Monday            | 0-6: Monday = 0, Tuesday = 1, ..., Sunday = 6  |\n| 2    | Sunday            | 0-6: Sunday = 0, Monday = 1, ..., Saturday = 6 |\n| 3    | Sunday            | 1-7: Sunday = 1, Monday = 2, ..., Saturday = 7 |\n\n### Returns\n\n- The day of the week (1-7), depending on the chosen mode, of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nThe following date is April 21, 2023, which was a Friday:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21')),\n    toDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21'), 1)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21'))─┬─toDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21'), 1)─┐\n│                                     5 │                                        4 │\n└───────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toHour\n\nExtracts the hour component (0-23) from a datetime value. This function accounts for daylight saving time changes based on typical rules.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoHour(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `HOUR`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The hour of the day (0 - 23) of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toHour(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toHour(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                        10 │\n└───────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toMinute\n\nExtracts the minute component (0-59) from a datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoMinute(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `MINUTE`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The minute of the hour (0 - 59) of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toMinute(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toMinute(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                          20 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toSecond\n\nExtracts the second component (0-59) from a datetime value, ignoring leap seconds.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoSecond(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `SECOND`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The second in the minute (0 - 59) of the given date/time. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toSecond(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toSecond(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                          30 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toMillisecond\n\nExtracts the millisecond component (0-999) from a datetime value.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoMillisecond(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `MILLISECOND`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The millisecond in the minute (0 - 59) of the given date/time. [UInt16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toMillisecond(toDateTime64('2023-04-21 10:20:30.456', 3))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌──toMillisecond(toDateTime64('2023-04-21 10:20:30.456', 3))─┐\n│                                                        456 │\n└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toUnixTimestamp\n\nConverts a date, datetime, or string representation of a date/time into a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch) as a `UInt32`. An optional timezone can be specified for string inputs.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUnixTimestamp(date)\ntoUnixTimestamp(str, [timezone])\n```\n\n### Returns\n\n- Returns the unix timestamp. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    '2017-11-05 08:07:47' AS dt_str,\n    toUnixTimestamp(dt_str) AS from_str,\n    toUnixTimestamp(dt_str, 'Asia/Tokyo') AS from_str_tokyo,\n    toUnixTimestamp(toDateTime(dt_str)) AS from_datetime,\n    toUnixTimestamp(toDateTime64(dt_str, 0)) AS from_datetime64,\n    toUnixTimestamp(toDate(dt_str)) AS from_date,\n    toUnixTimestamp(toDate32(dt_str)) AS from_date32\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\nRow 1:\n──────\ndt_str:          2017-11-05 08:07:47\nfrom_str:        1509869267\nfrom_str_tokyo:  1509836867\nfrom_datetime:   1509869267\nfrom_datetime64: 1509869267\nfrom_date:       1509840000\nfrom_date32:     1509840000\n```\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe return type of `toStartOf*`, `toLastDayOf*`, `toMonday`, `timeSlot` functions described below is determined by the configuration parameter enable_extended_results_for_datetime_functions which is `0` by default.\n\nBehavior for\n* `enable_extended_results_for_datetime_functions = 0`:\n  * Functions `toStartOfYear`, `toStartOfISOYear`, `toStartOfQuarter`, `toStartOfMonth`, `toStartOfWeek`, `toLastDayOfWeek`, `toLastDayOfMonth`, `toMonday` return `Date` or `DateTime`.\n  * Functions `toStartOfDay`, `toStartOfHour`, `toStartOfFifteenMinutes`, `toStartOfTenMinutes`, `toStartOfFiveMinutes`, `toStartOfMinute`, `timeSlot` return `DateTime`. Though these functions can take values of the extended types `Date32` and `DateTime64` as an argument, passing them a time outside the normal range (year 1970 to 2149 for `Date` / 2106 for `DateTime`) will produce wrong results.\n* `enable_extended_results_for_datetime_functions = 1`:\n  * Functions `toStartOfYear`, `toStartOfISOYear`, `toStartOfQuarter`, `toStartOfMonth`, `toStartOfWeek`, `toLastDayOfWeek`, `toLastDayOfMonth`, `toMonday` return `Date` or `DateTime` if their argument is a `Date` or `DateTime`, and they return `Date32` or `DateTime64` if their argument is a `Date32` or `DateTime64`.\n  * Functions `toStartOfDay`, `toStartOfHour`, `toStartOfFifteenMinutes`, `toStartOfTenMinutes`, `toStartOfFiveMinutes`, `toStartOfMinute`, `timeSlot` return `DateTime` if their argument is a `Date` or `DateTime`, and they return `DateTime64` if their argument is a `Date32` or `DateTime64`.\n{% /callout %}\n\n## toStartOfYear\n\nTruncates a date or datetime value to the beginning of its respective year.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfYear(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The first day of the year of the input date/time. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toStartOfYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toStartOfYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                       2023-01-01 │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfISOYear\n\nTruncates a date or datetime value to the first day of its ISO year, which may differ from the calendar year start.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfISOYear(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The first day of the year of the input date/time. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toStartOfISOYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toStartOfISOYear(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                          2023-01-02 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfQuarter\n\nTruncates a date or datetime value to the first day of its quarter (January 1, April 1, July 1, or October 1).\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfQuarter(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The first day of the quarter of the given date/time. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toStartOfQuarter(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toStartOfQuarter(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                          2023-04-01 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfMonth\n\nTruncates a date or datetime value to the first day of its month.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfMonth(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The first day of the month of the given date/time. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toStartOfMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toStartOfMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                        2023-04-01 │\n└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe behavior of parsing incorrect dates is implementation specific. The implementation may return zero date, throw an exception, or do \"natural\" overflow.\n{% /callout %}\n\n## toLastDayOfMonth\n\nAdjusts a date or datetime value to the last day of its month.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoLastDayOfMonth(value)\n```\n\nAlias: `LAST_DAY`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The last day of the month of the given date/time. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toLastDayOfMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toLastDayOfMonth(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                                          2023-04-30 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toMonday\n\nFinds the most recent Monday on or before the given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoMonday(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The date of the nearest Monday on or prior to the given date. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toMonday(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')), /* a Friday */\n    toMonday(toDate('2023-04-24')) /* already a Monday */\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toMonday(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┬─toMonday(toDate('2023-04-24'))─┐\n│                                  2023-04-17 │                     2023-04-24 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfWeek\n\nTruncates a date or datetime value to the beginning of its week, allowing specification of whether the week starts on Sunday or Monday.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfWeek(t[, mode[, timezone]])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `t`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `mode`: determines the first day of the week as described in the [toWeek()](#toweek) function.\n- `timezone`: Optional parameter, it behaves like any other conversion function.\n\n### Returns\n\n- The date of the nearest Sunday or Monday on or prior to the given date, depending on the mode. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toStartOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')), /* a Friday */\n    toStartOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'), 1), /* a Friday */\n    toStartOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-24')), /* a Monday */\n    toStartOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-24'), 1) /* a Monday */\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoStartOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')):    2023-04-16\ntoStartOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'), 1): 2023-04-17\ntoStartOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-24')):                 2023-04-23\ntoStartOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-24'), 1):              2023-04-24\n```\n\n## toLastDayOfWeek\n\nAdjusts a date or datetime value to the end of its week, allowing specification of whether the week ends on Saturday or Sunday.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoLastDayOfWeek(t[, mode[, timezone]])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `t`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `mode`: determines the last day of the week as described in the [toWeek](#toweek) function.\n- `timezone`: Optional parameter, it behaves like any other conversion function.\n\n### Returns\n\n- The date of the nearest Sunday or Monday on or after the given date, depending on the mode. [Date](../data-types/date).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toLastDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')), /* a Friday */\n    toLastDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'), 1), /* a Friday */\n    toLastDayOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-22')), /* a Saturday */\n    toLastDayOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-22'), 1) /* a Saturday */\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoLastDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')):    2023-04-22\ntoLastDayOfWeek(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'), 1): 2023-04-23\ntoLastDayOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-22')):                 2023-04-22\ntoLastDayOfWeek(toDate('2023-04-22'), 1):              2023-04-23\n```\n\n## toStartOfDay\n\nTruncates a datetime value to the very beginning of its day (midnight).\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfDay(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the day of the given date/time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toStartOfDay(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toStartOfDay(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┐\n│                             2023-04-21 00:00:00 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfHour\n\nTruncates a datetime value to the beginning of its hour.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfHour(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the hour of the given date/time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toStartOfHour(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')),\n    toStartOfHour(toDateTime64('2023-04-21', 6))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toStartOfHour(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30'))─┬─toStartOfHour(toDateTime64('2023-04-21', 6))─┐\n│                              2023-04-21 10:00:00 │                          2023-04-21 00:00:00 │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfMinute\n\nTruncates a datetime value to the beginning of its minute.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfMinute(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the minute of the given date/time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toStartOfMinute(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')),\n    toStartOfMinute(toDateTime64('2023-04-21 10:20:30.5300', 8))\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoStartOfMinute(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:30')):           2023-04-21 10:20:00\ntoStartOfMinute(toDateTime64('2023-04-21 10:20:30.5300', 8)): 2023-04-21 10:20:00\n```\n\n## toStartOfSecond\n\nRemoves any fractional second components from a `DateTime64` value, effectively rounding down to the nearest second.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfSecond(value, [timezone])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Date and time. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `timezone`: Timezone for the returned value (optional). If not specified, the function uses the timezone of the `value` parameter. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Input value without sub-seconds. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery without timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999', 3) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfSecond(dt64)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌───toStartOfSecond(dt64)─┐\n│ 2020-01-01 10:20:30.000 │\n└─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nQuery with timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999', 3) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfSecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─toStartOfSecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')─┐\n│                2020-01-01 13:20:30.000 │\n└────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfMillisecond\n\nTruncates a `DateTime64` value to the beginning of its millisecond.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfMillisecond(value, [timezone])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Date and time. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `timezone`: Timezone for the returned value (optional). If not specified, the function uses the timezone of the `value` parameter. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Input value with sub-milliseconds. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery without timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfMillisecond(dt64)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌────toStartOfMillisecond(dt64)─┐\n│ 2020-01-01 10:20:30.999000000 │\n└───────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nQuery with timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfMillisecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─toStartOfMillisecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')─┐\n│               2020-01-01 12:20:30.999000000 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfMicrosecond\n\nTruncates a `DateTime64` value to the beginning of its microsecond.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfMicrosecond(value, [timezone])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Date and time. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `timezone`: Timezone for the returned value (optional). If not specified, the function uses the timezone of the `value` parameter. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Input value with sub-microseconds. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery without timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌────toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64)─┐\n│ 2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999000 │\n└───────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nQuery with timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')─┐\n│               2020-01-01 12:20:30.999999000 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfNanosecond\n\nTruncates a `DateTime64` value to the beginning of its nanosecond.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfNanosecond(value, [timezone])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: Date and time. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `timezone`: Timezone for the returned value (optional). If not specified, the function uses the timezone of the `value` parameter. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Returns\n\n- Input value with nanoseconds. [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery without timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfNanosecond(dt64)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─────toStartOfNanosecond(dt64)─┐\n│ 2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999 │\n└───────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nQuery with timezone:\n\n```sql\nWITH toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9) AS dt64\nSELECT toStartOfNanosecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```text\n┌─toStartOfNanosecond(dt64, 'Asia/Istanbul')─┐\n│              2020-01-01 12:20:30.999999999 │\n└────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toStartOfFiveMinutes\n\nTruncates a datetime value to the start of the nearest five-minute interval.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfFiveMinutes(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the five-minute interval of the given date/time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toStartOfFiveMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:17:00')),\n    toStartOfFiveMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:00')),\n    toStartOfFiveMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:23:00'))\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoStartOfFiveMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:17:00')): 2023-04-21 10:15:00\ntoStartOfFiveMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:00')): 2023-04-21 10:20:00\ntoStartOfFiveMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:23:00')): 2023-04-21 10:20:00\n```\n\n## toStartOfTenMinutes\n\nTruncates a datetime value to the start of the nearest ten-minute interval.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfTenMinutes(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the ten-minute interval of the given date/time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toStartOfTenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:17:00')),\n    toStartOfTenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:00')),\n    toStartOfTenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:23:00'))\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoStartOfTenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:17:00')): 2023-04-21 10:10:00\ntoStartOfTenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:00')): 2023-04-21 10:20:00\ntoStartOfTenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:23:00')): 2023-04-21 10:20:00\n```\n\n## toStartOfFifteenMinutes\n\nTruncates a datetime value to the start of the nearest fifteen-minute interval.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfFifteenMinutes(value)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the fifteen-minute interval of the given date/time. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toStartOfFifteenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:17:00')),\n    toStartOfFifteenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:00')),\n    toStartOfFifteenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:23:00'))\nFORMAT Vertical\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoStartOfFifteenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:17:00')): 2023-04-21 10:15:00\ntoStartOfFifteenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:20:00')): 2023-04-21 10:15:00\ntoStartOfFifteenMinutes(toDateTime('2023-04-21 10:23:00')): 2023-04-21 10:15:00\n```\n\n## toStartOfInterval\n\nTruncates a date or datetime value to the beginning of a specified time interval (e.g., year, month, day, hour). This function offers flexible interval rounding, similar to other `toStartOf*` functions.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoStartOfInterval(value, INTERVAL x unit[, time_zone])\ntoStartOfInterval(value, INTERVAL x unit[, origin[, time_zone]])\n```\n\nAliases: `time_bucket`, `date_bin`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `value`: a [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `x`: The number of `unit` intervals. [Integer](../data-types/int-uint).\n- `unit`: The time unit for the interval. Possible values: `YEAR`, `QUARTER`, `MONTH`, `WEEK`, `DAY`, `HOUR`, `MINUTE`, `SECOND`, `MILLISECOND`, `MICROSECOND`, `NANOSECOND`.\n- `origin`: An optional reference point for the interval calculation. [DateTime](../data-types/datetime).\n- `time_zone`: Optional [Timezone](../data-types/string) for the returned value.\n\n### Returns\n\n- The start of the specified interval. [Date](../data-types/date), [Date32](../data-types/date32), [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) or [DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT toStartOfInterval(toDateTime('2023-01-01 14:45:00'), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE, toDateTime('2023-01-01 14:35:30'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌───toStartOfInterval(...)─┐\n│      2023-01-01 14:44:30 │\n└──────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toTime\n\nExtracts the time component from a date or datetime value and sets the date to `1970-01-02`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoTime(date[,timezone])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date to convert to a time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n- `timezone` (optional): Timezone for the returned value. [String](../data-types/string).\n\n### Returns\n\n- [DateTime](../data-types/datetime) with date equated to `1970-01-02` while preserving the time.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the `date` input argument contained sub-second components,\nthey will be dropped in the returned `DateTime` value with second-accuracy.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT toTime(toDateTime64('1970-12-10 01:20:30.3000',3)) AS result, toTypeName(result)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌──────────────result─┬─toTypeName(result)─┐\n│ 1970-01-02 01:20:30 │ DateTime           │\n└─────────────────────┴────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeYearNum\n\nCalculates the number of years that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeYearNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of years from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toRelativeYearNum(toDate('2002-12-08')) AS y1,\n    toRelativeYearNum(toDate('2010-10-26')) AS y2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌───y1─┬───y2─┐\n│ 2002 │ 2010 │\n└──────┴──────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeQuarterNum\n\nCalculates the number of quarters that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeQuarterNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of quarters from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  toRelativeQuarterNum(toDate('1993-11-25')) AS q1,\n  toRelativeQuarterNum(toDate('2005-01-05')) AS q2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌───q1─┬───q2─┐\n│ 7975 │ 8020 │\n└──────┴──────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeMonthNum\n\nCalculates the number of months that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeMonthNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of months from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  toRelativeMonthNum(toDate('2001-04-25')) AS m1,\n  toRelativeMonthNum(toDate('2009-07-08')) AS m2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌────m1─┬────m2─┐\n│ 24016 │ 24115 │\n└───────┴───────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeWeekNum\n\nCalculates the number of weeks that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeWeekNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of weeks from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  toRelativeWeekNum(toDate('2000-02-29')) AS w1,\n  toRelativeWeekNum(toDate('2001-01-12')) AS w2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌───w1─┬───w2─┐\n│ 1574 │ 1619 │\n└──────┴──────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeDayNum\n\nCalculates the number of days that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeDayNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of days from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  toRelativeDayNum(toDate('1993-10-05')) AS d1,\n  toRelativeDayNum(toDate('2000-09-20')) AS d2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌───d1─┬────d2─┐\n│ 8678 │ 11220 │\n└──────┴───────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeHourNum\n\nCalculates the number of hours that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeHourNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of hours from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  toRelativeHourNum(toDateTime('1993-10-05 05:20:36')) AS h1,\n  toRelativeHourNum(toDateTime('2000-09-20 14:11:29')) AS h2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─────h1─┬─────h2─┐\n│ 208276 │ 269292 │\n└────────┴────────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeMinuteNum\n\nCalculates the number of minutes that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeMinuteNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of minutes from a fixed reference point in the past. [UInt32](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  toRelativeMinuteNum(toDateTime('1993-10-05 05:20:36')) AS m1,\n  toRelativeMinuteNum(toDateTime('2000-09-20 14:11:29')) AS m2\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌───────m1─┬───────m2─┐\n│ 12496580 │ 16157531 │\n└──────────┴──────────┘\n```\n\n## toRelativeSecondNum\n\nCalculates the number of seconds that have passed since a predefined historical reference point for a given date or datetime.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoRelativeSecondNum(date)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `date`: Date or date with time. [Date](../data-types/date)/[DateTime](../data-types/datetime)/[DateTime64](../data-types/datetime64).\n\n### Returns\n\n- The number of seconds from a fixed reference point in the past.
